content.full_text:
City of Salinas
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY • 200 Lincoln Avenue • Salinas, California 93901 • (408) 758-7256

October 6, 1992

Jill Stecher, Counsel
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95812

RE: REQUEST FOR ADVICE
FILE NO. A-92-550

Dear Ms. Stecher:

In reviewing your advice letter with Councilmember Caballero, she
asked that I provide further clarification regarding the second
question posed in my letter dated August 4, 1992 in order to obtain
additional advice.

Ms. Caballero and I understand the award of the maintenance
contract may involve a conflict of interest depending on her
economic interests vis-a-vis the person or business entity
receiving the contract. The thrust of my request, however, was
focused on the Council's preliminary decision whether or not to
contract the work to outside contractors which must be decided
prior to the Council receiving specific information about the
particular contracting person or business entity.

In other words, does Ms. Caballero have a conflict of interest when
determining the preliminary question of whether the work should be
contracted through outside services or whether the City crew should
do the work? The decision whether to have the maintenance work
performed by City staff or by outside contractors could have an
impact on the maintenance cost, but not necessarily, if the City
and contractor costs are equal.

I hope that this correspondence adequately clarifies my second
question. I am available to provide additional information, if
needed, and I thank you for your continuing assistance and
courtesies.

Sincerely,

STEPHANIE A. ATIGH
CITY ATTORNEY

CC: Councilmember Anna Caballero


August 4, 1992


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

RE: REQUEST FOR FORMAL WRITTEN ADVICE

Dear Commission:

Pursuant to Government Code section 83114(b) and section 18329 of the California Code of Regulations, formal written advice is hereby requested as follows:

a. This request is made on behalf of Anna Caballero, Councilmember of the City of Salinas, c/o 200 Lincoln Avenue, Salinas, CA 93901. Ms. Caballero has authorized Stephanie Atigh, Salinas City Attorney, to submit this request for formal written advice to determine if she is disqualified from participating in or making decisions pursuant to the Political Reform Act.

b. The material facts for this request are as follows:

Ms. Caballero has been an elected member of the City Council of Salinas continuously since June, 1991. She and her husband own real estate at 941 New Salem Drive in Salinas. This real estate is located within the North East Assessment District, established by the City Council in 1989, to acquire and construct public improvements such as streets, curbs, gutters and sidewalks, street lights, storm drains, sanitary sewers, parks and open space. Ms. Caballero's real estate is also located in the North East Maintenance District which was established in 1990 for the purpose of maintaining the public improvements acquired and constructed by the North East Assessment District.

The City Council is required to make several determinations annually regarding the North East Assessment District. First, the Council is asked to determine how the maintenance work will be performed specifically, whether the work will be performed by in-house City staff or by outside contractors through competitive bid. If the Council determines that the maintenance work should be done by contract, the Council is then asked to confirm the plans and specifications of the proposed maintenance work and to advertise for bids to be returned by a specific date. Those bids are later brought to the Council for a decision regarding the award of the contract.

-1-


In addition, the assessments for the North East Maintenance
District are reviewed annually through a public hearing process at
which the Council is asked to confirm the assessments and levy
assessments for that fiscal year. Those assessments are then
levied against each parcel of land in the District, including the
real estate owned by Ms. Caballero, to be collected with the
payment of real property taxes in two annual installments.

Ms. Caballero has abstained from participating in any decision
regarding the annual assessments for the North East Maintenance
District. She has also recently abstained from a Council decision
to have the maintenance work performed by City staff as opposed to
an independent contractor. Ms. Caballero has expressed her
intention not to participate in any decision regarding the North
East Assessment District or North East Maintenance District pending
this request for formal written advice from the Commission.

I am available to provide additional information as needed.
Thank you for your assistance with this request.

Sincerely,

STEPHANIE A. ATIGH
CITY ATTORNEY

cc: Councilmember Anna Caballero


-2-
